question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $88,000 on November 22?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Crypto Market Flows | 20 November 2025
evidences.content: On 3 October, total Bitcoin futures open interest across major exchanges reached a record of roughly $90bn notional. Since then, open interest has fallen by about 30%, to roughly $65–70bn, signalling a material clearing-out of levered positions. CME Bitcoin futures open interest has similarly stepped down from last year’s highs, pointing to lighter institutional positioning. Perpetual futures open interest in BTC is down more than 20% in coin terms and over 30% in USD terms from the peak, with funding rates compressing sharply. The combination of ETF outflows and declining futures OI points to a market that has flushed a large portion of speculative leverage. Books are less crowded, but also more sensitive to fresh catalysts. ### Stablecoin Regime – Dollar Rails at Scale [...] On the Ethereum side: Spot ETH ETFs attracted strong initial demand, with multiple days of $200m+ net inflows across the complex around launch. In November, flows turned negative as investors rotated back toward BTC and reduced overall risk, with several days of sizeable net outflows. Takeaway: ETF channels have moved from one-way buying to two-way, price-sensitive flow. Capital is still sticky, but margin...

evidences.title: [PDF] Watching the FedWatch
evidences.content: Finally, there is a 100 bps rate hike in September and a 75 bps rate hike in November with a possibility of 0.5%. In summary, from the CME FedWatch Tool calculation, there are three possible outcomes after the November FOMC meeting. A total of 125 bps rate hike with 85% probability, a 150 bps total rate hike with 14.5% probability, and a 175 bps rate hike with only 0.5% probability. IA2 [...] For further details, see Section 5.3. 40 Tables Table 1: Summary of Statistics The data sample of unconditional probabilities of different potential target rate ranges for future FOMC meetings is constructed using daily Fed funds futures data from Bloomberg. Second, the daily yields of Treasury bills/bonds are obtained from the U.S. Department of the Treasury website, focusing on 3 and 6-month Treasury bills and the 1, 2, 5, 10, and 20-year Treasury bond yields to represent the yield curve. Third, the Python package yfinance is utilized to download daily SPY and VIX data from Yahoo Finance. [...] probabilities using the methodology outlined by the CME Group for their 6 FedWatch Tool and evaluates its accuracy. Our findings reveal that this straightforward binary tool achieves an accuracy of...

evidences.title: Bitcoin vs. the US Dollar: Unveiling Resilience Through Wavelet Analysis of Price Dynamics
evidences.content: In all the WTC heatmaps, the expected pattern of a strong dollar leading (with leftward arrows denoting a negative correlation) is generally evident, especially for global equities and gold, which show a consistently higher inverse relationship with the DXY. Commodities and oil exhibit lower overall coherence, likely because the dollar’s movements are influenced by inflationary pressures that also drive commodity prices. Ethereum, meanwhile, reveals surprisingly subdued coherence values; however, this observation must be viewed in light of Ethereum’s substantial correlation and coherence with BTC (Al-Mansouri et al., 2025) and its comparatively smaller market depth. Consequently, focusing on BTC’s price behavior may offer a clearer perspective on the reduced ETH–DXY coherence.
